across.clue: A situation in which everything is confused and in a mess.
According to everyone | By all accounts
To make a situation more confusing or complicated than it needs to be | ___ the waters
A victory or success that comes at such a great cost that it is effectively not worth winning | a ___ victory
Intended to be temporary and likely to be changed when other arrangements are made.
Make something seem uncertain | Cast ___ on
To improve a room or a building by cleaning and painting it and adding new furniture or equipment.
Following your command, instruction, or request; because you ordered or asked for it | at your ___
To speak or act in favour of a particular policy or way of doing things.
To take an irreversible step; to pass a point of no return in a decision or course of action | ___ the Rubicon

down.clue: To think carefully about something for a long time before reaching a decision.
Believing that people are only interested in themselves and are not sincere.
To officially accuse someone of a crime and ask a court of law to judge them.
Likely to have a good relationship because of being similar.
A disagreement about a public policy or moral issue that many people feel strongly about.
Someone in a position of authority | ___ holder
Happening when most needed or appropriate.
